# Code of Merit

This Code of Merit applies to all repositories, issues, pull requests, discussions, and other community spaces operated under the [drunikbe](https://github.com/drunikbe) GitHub organization. It is governed by the laws of Belgium. It does not override governing law or GitHub's Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Policies; where any conflict exists, those take precedence over this document.

## Merit

1. The project creators, lead developers, and core team constitute the managing members of the project. These members have final say in every decision of the project, technical or otherwise, including overruling previous decisions.

2. Contributions are an expected result of your membership on the project. Do not expect others to do your work, or to help you with it, indefinitely.

3. All members have the same opportunity to seek any challenge within the project.

4. Authority or position in the project is proportional to accrued contribution. Seniority must be earned.

5. Software is evolutionary: better implementations supersede lesser ones. Technical merit is the primary metric.

6. This is a space for technical work. Off-topic content may be moderated.

7. Non-technical conflicts belong in a separate space. Deliberate disruption of the project will not be allowed.

8. Individual characteristics — including but not limited to body, sex, sexual preference, race, language, religion, nationality, and political preference — are irrelevant to the scope of the project and will not be taken into account concerning your value or contribution.

9. Discuss or debate the idea, not the person.

## Communication

10. Provide context. Ambiguity will be met with requests for clarification; sustained failure to provide it will be met with silence. It is the responsibility of the originator to supply the requested context, and maintainers may decline or close work that lacks it.

11. Questioning, review, and requests for evidence or clarification are not harassment. Review is _civil_ when it addresses the code, proposal, evidence, or project impact and does not attack a participant's intelligence, motives, competence, identity, or character — "this implementation is incorrect because…" is permitted; "you are incompetent / naïve / dishonest" is not. Harassment is prohibited, including targeted intimidation, threats, stalking, sexualized attention, slurs, and repeated unwanted personal contact. After a participant has clearly asked to disengage from a discussion, continuing to contact that person about it is harassment regardless of tone or relevance; maintainers may still raise necessary project questions in the relevant project venue without directing them at that person. The difference is clear.

12. Constructive criticism will not be suppressed merely because it is negative, unpopular, or challenges a maintainer's decision. A constructive contribution states a project-relevant claim, reason, or proposed change. Removing, hiding, editing, locking, or limiting content is moderation — not censorship — when that content is unlawful, off-topic, personally abusive, threatening, discriminatory, spam, or materially disruptive to the work. Re-litigating a decision more than twice after a maintainer has formally closed it is disruptive. The difference is clear.

## Governance

13. Where clauses conflict, clauses 8 and 11–12, governing law, and platform policy take precedence over the operational clauses (5, 6, 7, 10). Technical merit is never a justification for conduct prohibited by clauses 8, 11, or 12.

14. Report violations privately to contact@drunik.be. Include links and relevant context. Reports about a managing member may be sent to any other managing member.

15. Managing members address violations proportionally, ordinarily escalating: (1) private reminder, (2) public warning citing the clause, (3) temporary mute or lock, or rejection of the contribution, (4) removal from role, (5) ban. Credible threats, illegal conduct, or platform abuse may skip steps. A managing member who is the subject of a report is recused from deciding it; the remaining managing members decide.

16. If something is illegal outside the scope of the project, it is illegal within the scope of the project.

17. This Code of Merit governs only the conduct of this project. Activities or objectives outside its scope are irrelevant; purposeful intent to deviate the project from its stated purpose is reason for remedial action. Participation on the project equates to agreement with this Code of Merit.

---

This document adapts the [Code of Merit](https://github.com/dpyro/Code-of-Merit) v1.0 with added conduct, governance, and enforcement provisions.
